alt_text: Figure 4.1 Composing two single-layer networks with three hidden units each. a) defines a function comprising three linear regions that take y and returns $y'$

text: Figure 4.1 Composing two single-layer networks with three hidden units each. a) defines a function comprising three linear regions that take y and returns $y'$ (i.e., the cyan circle is mapped to the brown circle). d) The combined effect of these two functions when composed is that (i) three different inputs x are mapped to any given value of y by the first network and (ii) are processed in the same way by the second network; the result is that the function defined by the second network defines
